passage:
The decision of the New York Philharmonic to hire Alan Gilbert as its
next music director has been the talk of the classical-music world ever
since the sudden announcement of his appointment in 2009. For the most
part, the response has been favorable, to say the least. "Hooray! At
last!" wrote Anthony Tommasini, a sober-sided classical-music critic.

One of the reasons why the appointment came as such a surprise, however,
is that Gilbert is comparatively little known. Even Tommasini, who had
advocated Gilbert's appointment in the *Times*, calls him "an
unpretentious musician with no air of the formidable conductor about
him." As a description of the next music director of an orchestra that
has hitherto been led by musicians like Gustav Mahler and Pierre Boulez,
that seems likely to have struck at least some *Times* readers as
faint praise.

For my part, I have no idea whether Gilbert is a great conductor or even
a good one. To be sure, he performs an impressive variety of interesting
compositions, but it is not necessary for me to visit Avery Fisher Hall,
or anywhere else, to hear interesting orchestral music. All I have to do
is to go to my CD shelf, or boot up my computer and download still more
recorded music from iTunes.

Devoted concertgoers who reply that recordings are no substitute for
live performance are missing the point. For the time, attention, and
money of the art-loving public, classical instrumentalists must compete
not only with opera houses, dance troupes, theater companies, and
museums, but also with the recorded performances of the great classical
musicians of the 20th century. These recordings are
cheap, available everywhere, and very often much higher in artistic
quality than today's live performances; moreover, they can be
"consumed" at a time and place of the listener's choosing. The
widespread availability of such recordings has thus brought about a
crisis in the institution of the traditional classical concert.

One possible response is for classical performers to program attractive
new music that is not yet available on record. Gilbert's own interest in
new music has been widely noted: Alex Ross, a classical-music critic,
has described him as a man who is capable of turning the Philharmonic
into "a markedly different, more vibrant organization." But what will
be the nature of that difference? Merely expanding the orchestra's
repertoire will not be enough. If Gilbert and the Philharmonic are to
succeed, they must first change the relationship between America's
oldest orchestra and the new audience it hopes to attract.

questions.questionText: We learn from Paragraph 1 that Gilbert's appointment has ____.
questions.question: We learn from Paragraph 1 that Gilbert's appointment has ____.
questions.explanation: 定位：P1: For the most part, the response has been favorable, to say the least. "Hooray! At last!"。第一段 favorable 和 Hooray 表明任命得到好评，对应 received acclaim，答案为 C。
questions.trapAnalysis: 干扰项主要来自局部词复现或对作者立场的误读。
questions.commonMistake: 常见错因：只看表面关键词，没有结合上下文和作者语气判断。
questions.location: P1: For the most part, the response has been favorable, to say the least. "Hooray! At last!"
questions.thinkingMethod: 先回到题干定位句，再把选项和原文做同义替换、逻辑和态度核对。

examinerThinking.sourceSentence: P1: For the most part, the response has been favorable, to say the least. "Hooray! At last!"
examinerThinking.trap: 干扰项主要来自局部词复现或对作者立场的误读。
examinerThinking.correctLogic: 第一段 favorable 和 Hooray 表明任命得到好评，对应 received acclaim，答案为 C。

questions.explanation: 定位：P2: calls him "an unpretentious musician with no air of the formidable conductor about him."。unpretentious 指不摆架子、谦逊，与 modest 同义，答案为 B。

questions.questionText: The author believes that the devoted concertgoers ____.
questions.question: The author believes that the devoted concertgoers ____.

options.A: ignore the expenses of live performances.
options.B: reject most kinds of recorded performances.
options.C: exaggerate the variety of live performances.
options.D: overestimate the value of live performances.

questions.explanation: 定位：P4: Devoted concertgoers ... are missing the point; recordings are cheap, available everywhere, and often higher in quality.。作者认为认定录音无法替代现场的人没有抓住重点，说明他们高估了现场演出价值，答案为 D。
questions.trapAnalysis: 干扰项主要来自局部词复现或对作者立场的误读。
questions.commonMistake: 常见错因：只看表面关键词，没有结合上下文和作者语气判断。

questions.questionText: According to the text, which of the following is true of recordings?
questions.question: According to the text, which of the following is true of recordings?

options.A: They are often inferior to live concerts in quality.
options.B: They are easily accessible to the general public.
options.C: They help improve the quality of music.

questions.explanation: 定位：P4: These recordings are cheap, available everywhere, and very often much higher in artistic quality than today's live performances.。available everywhere 对应 easily accessible to the general public，答案为 B。
questions.trapAnalysis: 干扰项主要来自局部词复现或对作者立场的误读。

questions.questionText: Regarding Gilbert's role in revitalizing the Philharmonic, the author feels ____.
questions.question: Regarding Gilbert's role in revitalizing the Philharmonic, the author feels ____.
questions.explanation: 定位：P5: But what will be the nature of that difference? Merely expanding the orchestra's repertoire will not be enough.。作者用反问和 will not be enough 表明对 Gilbert 振兴爱乐乐团的作用持怀疑态度，答案为 A。
questions.trapAnalysis: 干扰项主要来自局部词复现或对作者立场的误读。
